name: Gomeldon
overview: Gomeldon is a small village in the valley of the River Bourne, in the civil parish of Idmiston, about 5 miles north-east of Salisbury between Winterbourne Gunner and Porton. Its population was estimated at around 200 in 2012. The village has medieval origins and today has its own primary school; although there is no station, the West of England main line to London runs through the village.
character: A compact, quiet downland-edge village strung along the Bourne valley, mixing older cottages with later 20th-century housing and a family-friendly feel.
amenities: Recreation ground and Bourne Valley community activities
Bourne Valley Team Ministry churches in the neighbouring villages
No village shop or pub in Gomeldon itself - nearest are in the Winterbournes and Porton

commutes.notes: About 5 miles south-west for shops, services and the station; Old Sarum is about 3 miles
From Salisbury on South Western Railway; the West of England line runs through Gomeldon but there is no local station

prices.summary: Stock is a mix of period cottages and later 20th-century houses, giving relatively more family homes than the smaller valley hamlets; the village primary supports steady family demand. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking and cycling in the Bourne Valley and surrounding chalk downland
Visiting the Iron Age hill fort and former cathedral site at Old Sarum, about 3 miles away
Exploring the neighbouring Bourne Valley villages and their parish churches
placesToEat: The Winterbourne Arms at neighbouring Winterbourne Dauntsey
Pubs and facilities in nearby Porton and the Winterbournes
pros: Its own well-regarded village primary school
About 5 miles from Salisbury for a manageable commute
More of a mix of family housing than the surrounding hamlets
cons: No shop, pub or railway station in the village itself
Proximity to the main railway line and roads brings some background noise
whoItSuits: Families who want a village primary school and an easy run into Salisbury, and who do not need shops or a pub on the doorstep.
